Learn about the Master Service for manag: Learn about the Master Service for managing master data and settings in a multi-tenant environment.
The Master Service provides functionalit: The Master Service provides functionality for managing master data and settings in a multi-tenant environment
The Master Service consists of two main: The Master Service consists of two main components:
Supports creation of settings at all lev: Supports creation of settings at all levels
Provides update and delete operations fo: Provides update and delete operations for tenant settings
Implements CRUD operations for master da: Implements CRUD operations for master data entities
Ensures data integrity across tenant bou: Ensures data integrity across tenant boundaries
The solution for customizing the behavio: The solution for customizing the behavior of the `MasterModule` is to pass it an options `object` in the static `register()` method.
Enable or disable default master control: Enable or disable default master controller
Optional handlers to sync master data to: Optional handlers to sync master data to external systems (e.g., RDS)
Prisma service for RDS-backed queries. R: Prisma service for RDS-backed queries. Required when `enableController: true`
When `enableController: true`, the `pris: When `enableController: true`, the `prismaService` parameter is **required**. The module will throw an error at startup if `prismaService` is not provided when the controller is enabled.
The MasterSettingService interface manag: The MasterSettingService interface manages settings at various levels user, group, tenant, common. It allows retrieving, updating, creating, and deleting settings.
Retrieves a specific setting based on th: Retrieves a specific setting based on the provided setting code.
Creates a common tenant setting that is: Creates a common tenant setting that is shared across the system.
Creates a group-specific setting within: Creates a group-specific setting within a tenant.
Creates a user-specific setting within a: Creates a user-specific setting within a tenant.
Deletes a specific setting based on the: Deletes a specific setting based on the provided key.
Lists master settings with pagination an: Lists master settings with pagination and filtering. Requires RDS (Prisma) configuration.
Retrieves detailed master setting. Throw: Retrieves detailed master setting. Throws NotFoundException if not found.
Creates a new tenant setting. Wrapper fo: Creates a new tenant setting. Wrapper for createTenantSetting with automatic tenant code extraction from context.
`createBulk` internally calls `create` f: `createBulk` internally calls `create` for each item, which throws a `BadRequestException` if the setting already exists (e.g., `"Setting already exists: {code}"`). This method cannot be used to update existing settings. If you need upsert behavior (create or update), see the [Upsert Pattern](#upsert-pattern) section below.
Deletes a master setting. Wrapper for de: Deletes a master setting. Wrapper for deleteSetting.
Checks if a setting code already exists: Checks if a setting code already exists for the current tenant.
Copies master settings and data to other: Copies master settings and data to other tenants asynchronously using Step Functions. This is useful for initializing new tenants with existing master data.
Returns a task entity - the copy operati: Returns a task entity - the copy operation runs asynchronously
Data copy modes (used when copyType is D: Data copy modes (used when copyType is DATA_ONLY or BOTH):
`DataCopyMode.ALL`: Copies all master da: `DataCopyMode.ALL`: Copies all master data under the setting
The MasterDataService service provides m: The MasterDataService service provides methods to manage master data and operations. This includes listing, retrieving, creating, updating, and deleting data, as well as checking for the existence of specific codes.
Lists master data based on the provided: Lists master data based on the provided search criteria. Note: This method does not require an invoke context.
Get a master data by pk and sk.
Creates a new master data entity: Creates a new master data entity
Deletes specific master data based on th: Deletes specific master data based on the provided key.
Checks if a specific code exists within: Checks if a specific code exists within the given tenant and type.
Retrieves detailed master data including: Retrieves detailed master data including related information. Throws NotFoundException if not found.
Creates a new master data entity with au: Creates a new master data entity with automatic sequence generation if not provided.
`createSetting` throws a `BadRequestExce: `createSetting` throws a `BadRequestException` if the master data already exists (e.g., `"Master data already exists: {code}"`). If you need upsert behavior (create or update), see the [Upsert Pattern](#upsert-pattern) section below.
Creates multiple master data entities in: Creates multiple master data entities in bulk.
`createBulk` internally calls `createSet: `createBulk` internally calls `createSetting` for each item. It throws a `BadRequestException` if any item already exists. If you need upsert behavior, see the [Upsert Pattern](#upsert-pattern) section below.
Updates an existing master data entity.
Deletes a master data entity by key.
Searches master data in RDS with filteri: Searches master data in RDS with filtering and pagination. This method is used when Prisma service is configured.
Exact match for master type code: Exact match for master type code
In versions prior to v1.0.17, the `setti: In versions prior to v1.0.17, the `settingCode` parameter incorrectly used partial matching (`contains`) instead of exact matching. This caused unintended search results - for example, searching for "PRODUCT" would also return "PRODUCT_TYPE" and "MY_PRODUCT".
If you are using v1.0.16 or earlier and: If you are using v1.0.16 or earlier and need exact matching for `settingCode`, upgrade to v1.0.17 or later.
The framework provides built-in upsert m: The framework provides built-in upsert methods that automatically handle both creating new records and updating existing ones. These methods check DynamoDB for existing data and decide whether to create or update, skipping unchanged records for efficiency.
Built-in upsert methods (`upsert`, `upse: Built-in upsert methods (`upsert`, `upsertBulk`, `upsertSetting`, `upsertTenantSetting`) were added in [version 1.1.2](/docs/changelog#v112). In earlier versions, you need to implement custom upsert logic (see [Legacy Upsert Pattern](#legacy-upsert-pattern) below).
Creates or updates a tenant setting. If: Creates or updates a tenant setting. If the setting exists and has changes, it updates. If the setting exists but is unchanged, it returns the existing data without creating a new command. If the setting is deleted, it recreates it.
Wrapper for `upsertTenantSetting` with a: Wrapper for `upsertTenantSetting` with automatic tenant code extraction from context.
Upserts multiple settings sequentially. Items are processed one by one to avoid race conditions.
Creates or updates a master data entity. Behaves the same as `create` but does not throw when the record already exists.
Wrapper for `upsert` with automatic sequ: Wrapper for `upsert` with automatic sequence generation and tenant code extraction.
Upserts multiple master data entities se: Upserts multiple master data entities sequentially. Items are processed one by one to avoid seq race conditions.
When the controller is enabled (`enableC: When the controller is enabled (`enableController: true`), the framework provides a unified `/api/master-bulk/` endpoint that can handle both settings and data in a single request. Items are routed based on the presence of `settingCode`:
This item has settingCode → treated as m: This item has settingCode → treated as master data
This item has no settingCode → treated a: This item has no settingCode → treated as master setting
The response preserves the original inpu: The response preserves the original input order. Tenant code validation is enforced: if `tenantCode` is specified in an item, it must match the authenticated user's tenant.
Name of the setting or data: Name of the setting or data
Code of the setting or data: Code of the setting or data
Tenant code (must match authenticated us: Tenant code (must match authenticated user's tenant if specified)
If present, item is treated as master da: If present, item is treated as master data; otherwise as master setting
Attributes object. For settings, this is: Attributes object. For settings, this is used as settingValue
The upsert methods follow these rules:: The upsert methods follow these rules:
**New record**: If no existing record is: **New record**: If no existing record is found, creates a new one (version starts at 0)
**Existing record with changes**: If the: **Existing record with changes**: If the record exists and the input differs, updates it using the existing version number
**Existing record unchanged**: If the re: **Existing record unchanged**: If the record exists and the input is identical, skips the update and returns the existing data
**Deleted record**: If the record exists: **Deleted record**: If the record exists but is marked as deleted (`isDeleted: true`), recreates it using the existing version number
The custom upsert pattern below was requ: The custom upsert pattern below was required in versions prior to 1.1.2. For new projects, use the [built-in upsert methods](#upsert-pattern) instead.
For versions prior to 1.1.2, implement a: For versions prior to 1.1.2, implement a custom upsert service that checks for existing records before deciding whether to call create or update. See the [v1.1.1 documentation](https://github.com/mbc-net/mbc-cqrs-serverless/tree/v1.1.1) for the full legacy pattern.
